action.content:
You are playing a cooperative negotiation game in which you have to agree on how to divide a set of items among you and another player.

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the sum of your score and the score of the other player.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

1 pen, 1 flower, 3 frames

Your secret value function is:

pen: 4, flower: 0, frame: 2

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.
action.label: context

action.content: Hello! I'd like to work together to maximize our combined score. To do this efficiently, could you share which items you value most? I can tell you that I have strong preferences for some items and little interest in others. This way we can make sure the items go to whoever values them more.
action.label: response

pen: 2, flower: 5, frame: 1

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. Hello! I'd like to work together to maximize our combined score. To do this efficiently, could you share which items you value most? I can tell you that I have strong preferences for some items and little interest in others. This way we can make sure the items go to whoever values them more.
action.label: context

action.content:
I appreciate your collaborative approach! I'm happy to share my preferences to help us maximize our total score.

I value the items in this order: flower (highest), pen (medium), frames (lowest). The flower is significantly more valuable to me than the other items.

If you could share your preferences, we can figure out the best allocation. For example, if you value the pen or frames more than I do, it would make sense for you to take those, and I could take the flower. What are your priorities?
action.label: response

action.content:

action.content:
Perfect! Our preferences complement each other nicely. Here are my priorities:

- pen: highest value for me
- frames: medium value
- flower: lowest value (actually very little interest)

So the optimal allocation would be:
- You take the flower (your highest priority, my lowest)
- I take the pen (my highest priority, your medium)
- I take the 3 frames (my medium priority, your lowest)

This way, each item goes to whoever values it more, maximizing our total score. Does this allocation work for you?
action.label: response

- You take the pen and 3 frames
